tips.li: USAC uses Credit Bonds as its trading currency instead of the silver commonly used on the rim.
When trading with USAC merchants, make sure your Sealed Bags are inside Trade Beacon coverage.
Sealed Bags only store and transport corpses, so build cold storage to slow natural decay. USAC does not buy rotten corpses.
Sealed Bag value is affected by corpse durability and quality. Faster battlefield recovery and refrigeration are usually more profitable than leaving bodies outdoors.
Sealed Bag currency value only counts bags that already contain a corpse. Empty bags provide no trade value.
USAC uses Credit Bonds as its trading currency instead of the silver commonly used on the rim.
USAC prioritizes collecting bonds in trade. Buying new bonds with bonds may trigger an extra "depreciation fee," so store reserves in a vault and keep its Trade Beacon turned off.
The issued value of a Credit Bond is only 1000, but dealers usually sell at a premium. The premium depends on your faction goodwill with USAC.
Most USAC services consume bonds directly from Trade Beacon coverage instead of your entire map stockpile. Keep reserve bonds outside beacon range if needed.
If you run debt contracts and lease-style services on the same map, keep an extra bond buffer to avoid payment collisions on settlement days.
USAC charges debt interest once every 30 days as a quarter. Make sure your colony has enough Credit Bonds available on settlement day.
When repaying principal, any quarterly repayment above 10% of total principal can generate additional surcharge fees based on amount.
If paying down principal is difficult, at least maintain enough income to keep quarterly interest payments stable.
USAC offers two grace periods for unpaid interest, but the third default triggers forced collection.
If your debt is about to become unpayable, maybe consider escape this planet before too late.
Heavy Mining Rigs produce pollution continuously while operating. Early planning for cleanup and isolation zones reduces disruption to your colony core.
Heavy Mining Rig leases can be extended with bonds. If cash flow is tight, keep the rig running first and refill reserves in the next trade cycle.
Mech readiness decreases over time and causes penalties at very low levels. Stable resupply is usually more efficient than emergency refits.
Each mech resupply restores about 25% of max readiness. A dedicated resupply area and sufficient parts stock greatly reduce frontline downtime.
USAC airdropped units and equipment require valid landing space. If your base is crowded, reserve open tiles in advance to reduce drop failures or drift.
